## Canonical ownership check

- [ ] I identified which page in `docs/` owns the concept(s) this PR touches.
- [ ] If this introduces a new rule, it lives on its own page (or a clearly-scoped section of one), not folded into an unrelated page.
- [ ] I searched the repo for other places that might restate (rather than link to) the thing I changed, and updated them to link instead.
- [ ] If this changes a taxonomy/wording also mirrored in an Issue Form (`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/*.yml`) or `labels.yml`, I updated those too.

## Changelog

- [ ] This touches `docs/**` — the Changelog Automation workflow will auto-open a `type:changelog` issue on merge; I'll fill in Reason/Impact.
- [ ] This doesn't touch `docs/**` but is still worth recording — I opened a manual **Changelog Entry** issue.
- [ ] This is trivial (typo/formatting only) — commit message includes `[skip-changelog]`.
